registry_name: Five Whys Root-Cause Analysis Template (ICICLE)
proposed_title: Five Whys Root-Cause Analysis Template
proposed_description: Template for applying the Five Whys method to identify root causes of learning performance gaps during needs analysis and problem framing.
registry_description: Google Doc template for applying the Five Whys technique to identify root causes of learning performance gaps. Designed for the LE needs-analysis phase.

registry_name: Fishbone / Cause-Effect Analysis Template (ICICLE)

proposed_description: Ishikawa fishbone template for mapping contributing factors to learning problems in the discovery and diagnosis stages of learning engineering.
registry_description: Google Doc template for Ishikawa (fishbone) cause-and-effect diagrams in learning contexts. Used during LE problem framing and needs analysis to map contributing factors to learning gaps.

registry_name: Performance Task Analysis Template (ICICLE)
proposed_title: Performance Task Analysis Template
proposed_description: Structured task-analysis template that decomposes complex performance into observable subtasks to inform instructional and assessment design.
registry_description: Google Doc template for structured task decomposition in learning engineering. Guides practitioners through breaking complex performance into observable subtasks to inform instructional design.

registry_name: Learning Engineering Evidence Decision Tracker (ICICLE)
proposed_title: Learning Engineering Evidence Decision Tracker
proposed_description: Template set for documenting design decisions with their supporting evidence across iterations, improving transparency and reproducibility.
registry_description: Google Drive folder with templates for tracking design decisions alongside the evidence that motivated them throughout the LE process. Supports reproducible, evidence-based practice.

registry_name: Learning Engineering Implementation Checklist (ICICLE)
proposed_title: Learning Engineering Implementation Checklist
proposed_description: Project checklist covering end-to-end implementation steps from problem framing through evaluation and dissemination.
registry_description: Google Doc checklist for practitioners implementing a learning engineering project. Covers project phases from needs analysis through evaluation and dissemination.

registry_name: Examples of Roles & Expertise on an LE Team (ICICLE)
proposed_title: Learning Engineering Team Roles and Expertise Guide
proposed_description: Role map for multidisciplinary LE teams, outlining typical contributions from learning science, data, design, engineering, and domain experts.
registry_description: Google Doc describing the multidisciplinary roles typically needed on a learning engineering team: learning scientists, data analysts, instructional designers, engineers, subject matter experts.

registry_name: Learning Design Principles Toolkit (McEldoon, ICICLE)

registry_description: Google Drive toolkit by Katherine McEldoon connecting cognitive science principles to practical learning design decisions. Bridges learning science theory and LE design practice.

registry_name: QIP Learning Engineering Video Series (YouTube playlist)

proposed_description: Curated video series featuring LE practitioners and researchers discussing field foundations, implementation cases, and lessons learned.
registry_description: YouTube playlist produced by Quality Information Partners featuring practitioners and researchers discussing learning engineering principles, case studies, and field development.

registry_name: Invitation to Learning Engineering Webinar Series (ICICLE, YouTube)

proposed_description: ICICLE-hosted webinar series for newcomers, introducing core concepts, workflows, and entry points into the LE community.
registry_description: YouTube series of ICICLE-hosted webinars introducing the learning engineering field to new practitioners, covering foundational concepts, tools, and community resources.

registry_name: Silver Lining for Learning — Art & Science of Learning Engineering (podcast, 2023)

proposed_description: Podcast episode on balancing rigorous evidence practices with creative design in real-world learning engineering work.
registry_description: Episode of the Silver Lining for Learning podcast exploring learning engineering as both an art and a science — balancing rigorous measurement with creative instructional design.

registry_name: Silver Lining for Learning — Human-Centered Methods in LE (podcast, 2023)

registry_description: Episode of the Silver Lining for Learning podcast focused on human-centered design methods applied within the learning engineering process.

registry_name: Baker, Boser & Snow (2022) — Learning Engineering: A View on Where the Field Is At
proposed_title: Learning Engineering: A View on the Field and Future Research (Baker, Boser, Snow, 2022)
proposed_description: Open-access overview of the field’s current state, defining practices and outlining priority research problems for the next stage of development.
registry_description: Open-access article surveying the state of the learning engineering field as of 2022. Reviews definitions, practice norms, evidence standards, and key open problems.

registry_name: Craig et al. (2023) — Learning Engineering Perspectives for Educational Systems
proposed_title: Learning Engineering Perspectives for Educational Systems (Craig et al., 2023)
proposed_description: Article on how learning engineering can shape educational systems at scale through changes to policy, infrastructure, and practice.
registry_description: Multi-author article framing how learning engineering perspectives can transform educational systems at scale. Addresses policy, infrastructure, and community dimensions.

registry_name: Lee (2023) — Learning Sciences & Learning Engineering: Natural or Artificial Distinction?
proposed_title: Learning Sciences and Learning Engineering: Natural or Artificial Distinction? (Lee, 2023)
proposed_description: Scholarly analysis of the relationship between learning sciences and learning engineering, arguing for tighter integration of the two.
registry_description: Scholarly article examining the relationship between learning sciences and learning engineering. Argues for integration rather than separation, tracing epistemological differences and convergences.

registry_name: Kolodner (2023) — Learning Engineering: What It Is, Why I'm Involved
proposed_title: Learning Engineering: What It Is and Why I’m Involved (Kolodner, 2023)
proposed_description: Reflective essay on the origins, purpose, and practical value of learning engineering from a leading field contributor.
registry_description: Personal essay and scholarly reflection by Janet Kolodner on the nature of learning engineering, its roots in learning sciences, and her reasons for engagement with the field.

registry_name: AECT Learning Engagement Activated Podcast — Jim Goodell Episode (2022)

proposed_description: Podcast episode covering LE principles, ICICLE community work, and implications for evidence-based professional learning design.
registry_description: Podcast episode featuring Jim Goodell discussing learning engineering principles, the ICICLE community, and the future of evidence-based learning design in professional development contexts.

registry_name: Learning Engineering at a Glance (Schatz, Goodell & Kessler, Army Press, 2023)

registry_name: Learning Engineering is a Team Sport (ICICLE presentation, 2024)

registry_name: Teaming Up to Improve Medical & Healthcare Education (Kurzweil & Marcellas, 2020)

registry_name: Build a Learning Data Dream Team (Torrance, Lin & Goodell, ATD, 2024)
